lessons.title: Reading Files
lessons.concept_intro: Programs can read files from disk. The 'with open()' pattern safely opens and closes files.
lessons.analogy: Like opening a book, reading it, and putting it back on the shelf.

lessons.title: Writing Files
lessons.concept_intro: Programs can also write to files — saving data that persists after the program ends.
lessons.analogy: Like writing in a notebook — the notes are still there when you close it.

lessons.title: Try / Except
lessons.concept_intro: Sometimes things go wrong: file missing, bad input, network down. try/except lets you handle errors gracefully instead of crashing.
lessons.analogy: Like a safety net under a trapeze — if something goes wrong, you don't fall to the ground.

lessons.title: Common Exceptions
lessons.concept_intro: Different problems raise different exception types. Knowing them helps you catch the right ones.
lessons.analogy: Like knowing the difference between a flat tire and an empty tank — different problems, different solutions.

lessons.title: CSV & JSON
lessons.concept_intro: CSV and JSON are common formats for storing structured data. Python has built-in modules for both.
lessons.analogy: CSV is like a spreadsheet. JSON is like a dictionary written in a file.

project.title: Expense Tracker
project.description: Log expenses, categorize, save to CSV, show totals by category.
